text.heading: PDP convention fixed for March 8
text.p: From Johnchuks Onuanyim and Sanni Ologun Abuja
AFTER several postponements, the National Executive Committee (NEC) of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) yesterday fixed March 8 for the party’s convention.
The NEC also fixed February 21, 25, 28 and March 4 for the ward, local government, state and zonal congresses.
The party’s National Secretary, Chief Bernart Ezeh, said the Independent National Commission (INEC) would be notified of the dates.

Rising from its meeting in Abuja which brought together estranged members, such as former President Olusegun Obasanjo, Alhaji Abubakar Rimi and Chief Tony Anenih, the NEC readmitted 33 expelled members.
Four, Senator Ifeanyi Araume, , Senator Joel Ikenya, D. F. Maduabum and Ndudi Elumelu, who are members of the House of Representatives, were readmitted.
The fate of the 29 others, it said, would be decided by their states.
President Umaru Yar’Adua appealed to those interested in party office, to abide by the rule of law.

"We have a duty to ensure that these commitments also form the foundation of our conduct in the political arena. This is the only way we can meaningfully strengthen our party and the entire the national political process.

"We need to remind ourselves as we approach the national congresses and convention of the party that we must respect and uphold the rules, regulations and procedures as laid down by our great party. Party cohesion can be sustained within the contest of the defining rules and regulations that allow for fairness at all levels, he said.
The PDP chairman, Senator Ahmadu Ali, disagreed that the reforms introduced by former Obasanjo had been jettisoned by his successor, President Umaru Musa Yar’Adua.
Ali, said what was being mistaken for a dumping of the reforms was the adjustment of some of the policies.
He said: "Let me use this opportunity to dispel fears in some quarters that the economic and positive reforms are being reversed.
"The reforms started by the previous PDP government are on course and all good aspects are being sustained and deepened.
"For reforms to succeed, however, they need to be constantly monitored and necessary adjustments made from time to time.
"Our stance on anti-corruption remains on course; the approach may be modified for more effective and smoother operation."
The meeting that started at noon was also attended by former Vice President Alex Ekwueme, Barnabas Gemade, Kassim Ibrahim Usman.
Ken Nnamani, Pius Anyim Pius, Aminu Masari, Senate President David Mark and Speaker Dimeji Bankole among others.
Deputy National chairman (South) of the party (PDP) Chief Olabode George said Olusegun Obasanjo remaind the Chairman Board of Trustee (BOT) notwithstanding the forthcoming convention.
However Rimi declared that Obasanjo did not deserve to continue as BOT chairman became according to him, Obasanjo has "destroyed the party" and "done a lot of filthy things."
